desc: 100 frases reais de reuniões, apresentações, negociações e e-mails. Do hospital à McKinsey.

cards.term: Let's make sure we use our time well today.
cards.ph: /lets meɪk ʃɔːr wiː juːz ˈaʊər taɪm wel təˈdeɪ/
cards.pt: Vamos garantir que usamos nosso tempo bem hoje.
cards.def: An opening statement that sets an expectation of focus and efficiency before a meeting begins.
cards.ex: "We have a lot to cover and limited time — let's make sure we use our time well today."
cards.tip: Sinaliza que você respeita o tempo de todos. Abre permissão implícita para cortar tangentes.

cards.term: Can everyone see the agenda?
cards.ph: /kæn ˈevriwʌn siː ðə əˈdʒendə/
cards.pt: Todos conseguem ver a pauta?
cards.def: A practical opening question that confirms shared context and signals that the meeting is structured.
cards.ex: "Before we start — can everyone see the agenda? We have three items: status, scope decision, and next steps."
cards.tip: Meetings com agenda publicada terminam 40% mais rápido. Quem facilita a agenda, controla a narrativa.

cards.term: Let's take this offline.
cards.ph: /lets teɪk ðɪs ˈɒflaɪn/
cards.pt: Vamos resolver isso em separado, fora da reunião.
cards.def: Said to move a detailed or contentious topic out of the current meeting to a smaller, more appropriate setting.
cards.ex: "That's a valid point — let's take this offline and I'll loop you in before end of day."
cards.tip: Essencial para proteger o foco coletivo. Sinaliza que você valoriza o tema sem sequestrar a reunião.

cards.term: I want to make sure we're aligned on the next steps.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə meɪk ʃɔːr wiːr əˈlaɪnd ɒn ðə nekst steps/
cards.pt: Quero garantir que estamos alinhados nos próximos passos.
cards.def: A closing phrase confirming shared understanding of action items, owners, and deadlines before ending a meeting.
cards.ex: "Before we wrap up — I want to make sure we're aligned on next steps: Sarah owns the deck, deadline Friday."
cards.tip: Evita o clássico problema de todo mundo sair com entendimentos diferentes. Sempre feche reuniões assim.

cards.term: Who is the decision-maker on this?
cards.ph: /huː ɪz ðə dɪˈsɪʒən meɪkər ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Quem é o tomador de decisão nisso?
cards.def: A direct question clarifying accountability and authority before spending time on debate without a clear owner.
cards.ex: "Before we debate this further — who is the decision-maker on this? We need one person to own the call."
cards.tip: Clareza de RACI antes de qualquer debate poupa horas. Quem não sabe quem decide, não decide nada.

cards.term: Let's park that for now and come back to it.
cards.ph: /lets pɑːk ðæt fɔːr naʊ ænd kʌm bæk tə ɪt/
cards.pt: Vamos deixar isso de lado por agora e voltamos depois.
cards.def: A facilitation phrase used to acknowledge a topic without letting it derail the current agenda.
cards.ex: "That's worth exploring — let's park that for now and come back to it in the last ten minutes."
cards.tip: 'Parking lot' é uma técnica real de facilitação. Demonstra que você ouviu, sem perder o fio condutor.

cards.term: Can we do a quick round of introductions?
cards.ph: /kæn wiː duː ə kwɪk raʊnd əv ˌɪntrəˈdʌkʃənz/
cards.pt: Podemos fazer uma rodada rápida de apresentações?
cards.def: A standard opening in cross-functional or external meetings to ensure everyone knows who is in the room.
cards.ex: "We have a few new faces today — can we do a quick round of introductions before we dive in?"
cards.tip: Essencial em reuniões multidisciplinares. Cria contexto de quem fala com qual autoridade.

cards.term: I'd like to set some ground rules for today.
cards.ph: /aɪd laɪk tə set sʌm ɡraʊnd ruːlz fɔːr təˈdeɪ/
cards.pt: Gostaria de estabelecer algumas regras básicas para hoje.
cards.def: An opening statement that defines expected behaviors — cameras on, one conversation at a time, decisions recorded.
cards.ex: "Before we start — I'd like to set some ground rules for today: cameras on, one voice at a time, no phones."
cards.tip: Quem define as regras controla o ambiente. Sinaliza liderança de facilitação desde os primeiros segundos.

cards.term: Let's timebox this discussion to ten minutes.
cards.ph: /lets ˈtaɪmbɒks ðɪs dɪsˈkʌʃən tə ten ˈmɪnɪts/
cards.pt: Vamos limitar essa discussão a dez minutos.
cards.def: A time management technique that bounds a discussion to prevent overrun and maintain meeting momentum.
cards.ex: "We have five agenda items — let's timebox this to ten minutes and then move on."
cards.tip: 'Timebox' vem do Agile mas é usado em qualquer meeting profissional. Quem timeboxa, lidera.

cards.term: Are there any blockers I should know about?
cards.ph: /ɑːr ðeər ˈeni ˈblɒkərz aɪ ʃʊd nəʊ əˈbaʊt/
cards.pt: Há algum impedimento que eu deva saber?
cards.def: A direct check-in question in status meetings to surface obstacles early before they escalate.
cards.ex: "Quick check-in before we move on — are there any blockers I should know about on the integration track?"
cards.tip: Cria ambiente seguro para reportar problemas. Bloqueios escondidos afundam projetos.

cards.term: I want to make sure everyone has a chance to speak.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə meɪk ʃɔːr ˈevriwʌn hæz ə tʃɑːns tə spiːk/
cards.pt: Quero garantir que todos tenham chance de falar.
cards.def: An inclusive facilitation phrase that invites quieter participants and distributes voice in a meeting.
cards.ex: "We've heard a few strong perspectives — I want to make sure everyone has a chance to speak before we decide."
cards.tip: Mostra liderança inclusiva. Em consultoria, a perspectiva silenciosa frequentemente é a mais importante.

cards.term: Let's summarize what we agreed on before we close.
cards.ph: /lets ˈsʌməraɪz wɒt wiː əˈɡriːd ɒn bɪˈfɔːr wiː kləʊz/
cards.pt: Vamos resumir o que acordamos antes de encerrar.
cards.def: A closing discipline that verbally confirms all decisions and commitments made during the meeting.
cards.ex: "Two minutes left — let's summarize what we agreed on before we close so there's no ambiguity."
cards.tip: A reunião não termina com 'ok, bye'. Termina com clareza. Isso diferencia facilitador de participante.

cards.term: What's the headline here?
cards.ph: /wɒts ðə ˈhedlaɪn hɪər/
cards.pt: Qual é o ponto principal? A mensagem-chave?
cards.def: A direct request for the most important insight or conclusion, cutting through detail to get to the core message.
cards.ex: "We have three minutes before the CEO joins — what's the headline here?"
cards.tip: Clássico de consultoria. Ligado ao Pyramid Principle da McKinsey: conclusão primeiro, evidência depois.

cards.term: The bottom line is this.
cards.ph: /ðə ˈbɒtəm laɪn ɪz ðɪs/
cards.pt: A conclusão é a seguinte. O que importa é:
cards.def: A phrase that signals you are about to state the most critical point after providing context or background.
cards.ex: "I know we've covered a lot of data today — the bottom line is this: we need to act in the next 30 days."
cards.tip: Líderes ocupados precisam da conclusão antes dos dados. 'Bottom line' entrega isso sem ambiguidade.

cards.term: Let me give you the thirty-thousand-foot view.
cards.ph: /let miː ɡɪv juː ðə ˈθɜːti ˈθaʊzənd fʊt vjuː/
cards.pt: Deixa eu te dar a visão panorâmica, o contexto geral.
cards.def: An offer to provide a high-level, strategic overview before going into operational or analytical details.
cards.ex: "Before we dive into the numbers — let me give you the thirty-thousand-foot view of where the program stands."
cards.tip: Mostra domínio do contexto macro. Em apresentações executivas, sempre macro antes do micro.

cards.term: I'll take you through three key points.
cards.ph: /aɪl teɪk juː θruː θriː kiː pɔɪnts/
cards.pt: Vou apresentar três pontos-chave.
cards.def: An organizing statement that frames the structure of a presentation and sets audience expectations upfront.
cards.ex: "Good morning — I'll take you through three key points: where we are, what we recommend, and what we need from you."
cards.tip: Estrutura em três pontos é o padrão de clareza executiva. Quem não estrutura a fala, perde o ouvinte.

cards.term: The data tells a compelling story.
cards.ph: /ðə ˈdeɪtə telz ə kəmˈpelɪŋ ˈstɔːri/
cards.pt: Os dados mostram um cenário convincente, contam uma história clara.
cards.def: A phrase used to introduce a data-driven argument, framing numbers as a narrative rather than a data dump.
cards.ex: "The data tells a compelling story — every metric points to the same structural issue in the intake process."
cards.tip: Dados sem narrativa são ruído. Consultores transformam números em histórias que levam à ação.

cards.term: Our recommendation rests on three assumptions.
cards.ph: /ˈaʊər ˌrekəmenˈdeɪʃən rests ɒn θriː əˈsʌmpʃənz/
cards.pt: Nossa recomendação se apoia em três premissas.
cards.def: A transparent framing statement that explicitly names the assumptions behind a recommendation, building credibility.
cards.ex: "Before you approve this — our recommendation rests on three assumptions I want to be upfront about."
cards.tip: Transparência sobre premissas = credibilidade. Ocultar premissas = risco de colapso na implementação.

cards.term: With that context in mind, here is our proposal.
cards.ph: /wɪð ðæt ˈkɒntekst ɪn maɪnd hɪər ɪz ˈaʊər prəˈpəʊzəl/
cards.pt: Tendo esse contexto em mente, aqui está nossa proposta.
cards.def: A transition phrase that links the diagnostic analysis to the recommendation, connecting problem to solution.
cards.ex: "We've walked you through root causes — with that context in mind, here is our proposal."
cards.tip: O pivô da apresentação consultiva. Tudo antes é contexto. Tudo depois é proposta. Esta frase é a charneira.

cards.term: We modeled three scenarios.
cards.ph: /wiː ˈmɒdəld θriː sɪˈnɪəriəʊz/
cards.pt: Modelamos três cenários.
cards.def: An introduction to a scenario analysis that shows rigor and acknowledges uncertainty in projections.
cards.ex: "Rather than a single forecast, we modeled three scenarios: base case, upside, and stress case."
cards.tip: Análise de cenários mostra maturidade analítica. Evita parecer ingênuo ao apresentar projeção única.

cards.term: I want to be transparent about the risks.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə biː trænsˈpærənt əˈbaʊt ðə rɪsks/
cards.pt: Quero ser transparente sobre os riscos.
cards.def: A candid statement that proactively addresses potential downsides before the audience raises them.
cards.ex: "Before we finalize the recommendation — I want to be transparent about the risks we identified."
cards.tip: Consultores que só falam do upside perdem credibilidade. Nomear o risco primeiro é uma vantagem.

cards.term: Are there any questions before I move on?
cards.ph: /ɑːr ðeər ˈeni ˈkwestʃənz bɪˈfɔːr aɪ muːv ɒn/
cards.pt: Há alguma pergunta antes de eu continuar?
cards.def: A structured check-in during a presentation that ensures comprehension before advancing.
cards.ex: "That covers the diagnosis — are there any questions before I move on to the recommendation?"
cards.tip: Apresentadores inseguros evitam perguntas durante a apresentação. Experientes as convidam.

cards.term: What we're asking from you today is a decision on this.
cards.ph: /wɒt wiːr ˈɑːskɪŋ frɒm juː təˈdeɪ ɪz ə dɪˈsɪʒən ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: O que pedimos de vocês hoje é uma decisão sobre isso.
cards.def: A direct ask that clearly names what the presenter needs from the audience — a decision, not just a discussion.
cards.ex: "What we're asking from you today is a decision on whether to proceed with Phase 2."
cards.tip: Nunca saia de reunião executiva sem ask claro. Reunião sem ask é relatório com pessoas na sala.

cards.term: Let me bring this back to the strategic question.
cards.ph: /let miː brɪŋ ðɪs bæk tə ðə strəˈtiːdʒɪk ˈkwestʃən/
cards.pt: Deixa eu trazer isso de volta para a questão estratégica.
cards.def: A redirect phrase used when discussion drifts into operational detail, pulling it back to the core strategic issue.
cards.ex: "Those are valid operational concerns — but let me bring this back to the strategic question."
cards.tip: Operações são meios. Estratégia é o fim. Mantenha o fim em vista sempre.

cards.term: Can we put a number on that?
cards.ph: /kæn wiː pʊt ə ˈnʌmbər ɒn ðæt/
cards.pt: Conseguimos quantificar isso?
cards.def: A request to attach a specific, measurable figure to a claim, risk, or opportunity instead of leaving it qualitative.
cards.ex: "You said the delay creates 'significant risk' — can we put a number on that? What's the financial exposure?"
cards.tip: Transforma percepções vagas em dados acionáveis. Pergunta frequente de consultores e CFOs.

cards.term: The numbers tell a different story.
cards.ph: /ðə ˈnʌmbərz tel ə ˈdɪfrənt ˈstɔːri/
cards.pt: Os números mostram uma realidade diferente, contradizem o que foi dito.
cards.def: A phrase used to introduce data that contradicts a narrative or assumption being presented.
cards.ex: "The team believes we're outperforming, but the numbers tell a different story — churn is up 18%."
cards.tip: Contestar com dados, não com opinião. Atribui o argumento à evidência, reduz conflito pessoal.

cards.term: Let's pressure-test this assumption.
cards.ph: /lets ˈpreʃər test ðɪs əˈsʌmpʃən/
cards.pt: Vamos questionar e testar essa premissa com rigor.
cards.def: To rigorously examine and challenge a key assumption to see if it holds under scrutiny.
cards.ex: "The entire model hinges on 12% growth — we need to pressure-test this assumption before the board."
cards.tip: Mostra rigor analítico. Muito usado antes de apresentar recomendações a stakeholders céticos.

cards.term: What are the key drivers of this result?
cards.ph: /wɒt ɑːr ðə kiː ˈdraɪvərz əv ðɪs rɪˈzʌlt/
cards.pt: Quais são os principais fatores que explicam esse resultado?
cards.def: An analytical question seeking to identify the root causes or primary variables behind a metric or outcome.
cards.ex: "Revenue dropped 15% — what are the key drivers of this result? Volume, price, or mix?"
cards.tip: 'Driver' é a unidade de análise em consultoria. Decomposição de drivers é o começo de qualquer diagnóstico.

cards.term: How sensitive is this to changes in the assumptions?
cards.ph: /haʊ ˈsensɪtɪv ɪz ðɪs tə ˈtʃeɪndʒɪz ɪn ðə əˈsʌmpʃənz/
cards.pt: Qual é a sensibilidade disso a mudanças nas premissas?
cards.def: A sensitivity analysis question that tests how robust a conclusion is when inputs change.
cards.ex: "How sensitive is this to changes in the assumptions on cost of capital? What breaks the model?"
cards.tip: Sensitivity analysis = maturidade financeira. Toda projeção muda com as premissas. Sempre pergunte isso.

cards.term: Can you walk me through your thinking?
cards.ph: /kæn juː wɔːk miː θruː jɔːr ˈθɪŋkɪŋ/
cards.pt: Pode me explicar o raciocínio por trás disso?
cards.def: A polite and curious way to ask someone to explain the logic behind a decision, recommendation, or analysis.
cards.ex: "Before we commit to this direction — can you walk me through your thinking on the risk assumptions?"
cards.tip: Preferível a 'Why did you do this?' — muito mais colaborativo. Sinaliza curiosidade genuína, não crítica.

cards.term: Let's triangulate this with another data source.
cards.ph: /lets traɪˈæŋɡjʊleɪt ðɪs wɪð əˈnʌðər ˈdeɪtə sɔːs/
cards.pt: Vamos validar isso com outra fonte de dados.
cards.def: A methodological suggestion to confirm findings by cross-referencing with a second, independent source.
cards.ex: "The survey says 70% satisfaction — let's triangulate this with the NPS data before we report to leadership."
cards.tip: Uma fonte é anedota. Duas são coincidência. Três são padrão. Triangule sempre.

cards.term: What's the order of magnitude here?
cards.ph: /wɒts ðə ˈɔːdər əv ˈmæɡnɪtjuːd hɪər/
cards.pt: Qual é a grandeza, a escala disso?
cards.def: A question asking for a rough sense of scale rather than precision, to assess if something is material.
cards.ex: "I don't need the exact figure — what's the order of magnitude here? Thousands or millions?"
cards.tip: Consultores são rápidos em escala. A grandeza antes do detalhe economiza análise desnecessária.

cards.term: Correlation is strong, but causation is harder to prove.
cards.ph: /ˌkɒrəˈleɪʃən ɪz strɒŋ bʌt kɔːˈzeɪʃən ɪz ˈhɑːdər tə pruːv/
cards.pt: A correlação é forte, mas a causalidade é mais difícil de provar.
cards.def: A statistical caveat distinguishing correlation from causation — a critical analytical safeguard.
cards.ex: "The correlation is strong — but causation is harder to prove. We need to rule out confounding variables first."
cards.tip: A armadilha #1 de análise de dados. Confundir correlação e causalidade destrói credibilidade.

cards.term: What would change your mind on this?
cards.ph: /wɒt wʊd tʃeɪndʒ jɔːr maɪnd ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: O que mudaria sua opinião sobre isso?
cards.def: A Socratic question that forces someone to articulate the conditions under which they would revise their position.
cards.ex: "You seem unconvinced — what would change your mind? What evidence would you need to see?"
cards.tip: Poderosa em negociação e análise. Revela o critério real de decisão do interlocutor.

cards.term: This is a leading indicator, not a lagging one.
cards.ph: /ðɪs ɪz ə ˈliːdɪŋ ˈɪndɪkeɪtər nɒt ə ˈlæɡɪŋ wʌn/
cards.pt: Esse é um indicador antecedente, não retrospectivo.
cards.def: A distinction between predictive metrics (leading) and historical performance metrics (lagging).
cards.ex: "Watch the pipeline, not just closed deals — this is a leading indicator of where revenue will be in 90 days."
cards.tip: Leading indicators permitem agir antes do problema. Lagging confirmam o que já aconteceu.

cards.term: We need to separate signal from noise.
cards.ph: /wiː niːd tə ˈsepəreɪt ˈsɪɡnəl frɒm nɔɪz/
cards.pt: Precisamos separar o sinal do ruído.
cards.def: An analytical discipline of distinguishing meaningful trends from random variation or irrelevant data points.
cards.ex: "There's a lot of data coming in — we need to separate signal from noise and focus on what actually matters."
cards.tip: Metáfora de teoria da informação. Todo analista sênior pergunta: 'Isso é sinal ou ruído?' antes de agir.

cards.term: Let me push back on that for a second.
cards.ph: /let miː pʊʃ bæk ɒn ðæt fɔːr ə ˈsekənd/
cards.pt: Deixa eu questionar isso por um momento. Discordo respeitosamente.
cards.def: A polite but assertive way to express disagreement without being aggressive.
cards.ex: "I want to push back on that for a second — last quarter's data suggests a different pattern."
cards.tip: 'For a second' suaviza a resistência. Essencial em ambientes onde discordância direta é difícil.

cards.term: I hear you, and I see it differently.
cards.ph: /aɪ hɪər juː ænd aɪ siː ɪt ˈdɪfrəntli/
cards.pt: Entendo sua perspectiva, mas vejo de forma diferente.
cards.def: A conflict phrase that validates the other party's view before asserting a different one.
cards.ex: "I hear you on the urgency — and I see it differently. The risk of moving too fast outweighs the delay risk."
cards.tip: Validate first, then diverge. Estrutura da Comunicação Não-Violenta e da Negociação Harvard.

cards.term: That's a fair point. Here is the other side of it.
cards.ph: /ðæts ə feər pɔɪnt hɪər ɪz ðə ˈʌðər saɪd əv ɪt/
cards.pt: Esse é um ponto justo. Aqui está o outro lado.
cards.def: A balanced response that acknowledges merit in an opposing argument before presenting a counterpoint.
cards.ex: "That's a fair point about the timeline risk. Here is the other side — delay also has a real cost."
cards.tip: Conceder o que é verdade no argumento do outro fortalece sua posição. Harvard Negotiation 101.

cards.term: What would it take to get to yes?
cards.ph: /wɒt wʊd ɪt teɪk tə ɡet tə jes/
cards.pt: O que seria necessário para chegarmos a um 'sim'?
cards.def: A solution-oriented negotiation question that moves from stated positions to underlying interests.
cards.ex: "You've flagged concerns about cost and timing — what would it take to get to yes on the core proposal?"
cards.tip: Referência direta ao livro Getting to Yes (Fisher & Ury). Foca em interesses, não em posições.

cards.term: I want to understand your concerns before we move forward.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə ʌndəˈstænd jɔːr kənˈsɜːnz bɪˈfɔːr wiː muːv ˈfɔːwəd/
cards.pt: Quero entender suas preocupações antes de seguirmos em frente.
cards.def: An empathetic statement that creates space for resistance to be heard before pushing toward a decision.
cards.ex: "I notice some hesitation — I want to understand your concerns before we move forward."
cards.tip: Resistência não enfrentada sabota implementação. Melhor surfar a objeção do que ignorá-la.

cards.term: Let's find a middle ground.
cards.ph: /lets faɪnd ə ˈmɪdəl ɡraʊnd/
cards.pt: Vamos encontrar um meio-termo.
cards.def: An invitation to negotiate toward a compromise position that partially satisfies both parties' interests.
cards.ex: "Neither position is fully workable — let's find a middle ground that both teams can commit to."
cards.tip: Meio-termo não é fraqueza — é ZOPA (Zone of Possible Agreement) na prática.

cards.term: Can we agree to disagree on this one?
cards.ph: /kæn wiː əˈɡriː tə dɪsəˈɡriː ɒn ðɪs wʌn/
cards.pt: Podemos concordar em discordar nisso?
cards.def: A phrase that closes an irresolvable disagreement respectfully, without forcing artificial consensus.
cards.ex: "We've spent 20 minutes on this — can we agree to disagree and note both views in the minutes?"
cards.tip: Saber quando parar um debate é uma habilidade. Não toda discordância precisa de resolução.

cards.term: I need to understand the constraints on your side.
cards.ph: /aɪ niːd tə ʌndəˈstænd ðə kənˈstreɪnts ɒn jɔːr saɪd/
cards.pt: Preciso entender as restrições do seu lado.
cards.def: A question that seeks to understand the limitations, pressures, or boundaries the other party operates under.
cards.ex: "Before I respond to the proposal — I need to understand the constraints on your side: budget, timeline?"
cards.tip: BATNA começa pelo entendimento das restrições do outro. Negocie com informação, não suposições.

cards.term: What's your walk-away position?
cards.ph: /wɒts jɔːr ˈwɔːkəweɪ pəˈzɪʃən/
cards.pt: Qual é sua posição de recuo, seu limite mínimo aceitável?
cards.def: A direct question about the minimum acceptable outcome below which a party would rather walk away from a deal.
cards.ex: "We're close but not there yet — what's your walk-away position so we know if there is a deal to be made?"
cards.tip: Equivalente a perguntar pelo BATNA alheio. Só funciona com trust já estabelecido.

cards.term: I'm going to flag this as a risk.
cards.ph: /aɪm ˈɡəʊɪŋ tə flæɡ ðɪs æz ə rɪsk/
cards.pt: Vou registrar isso como um risco.
cards.def: A formal statement of intent to document a concern in a risk register or meeting record.
cards.ex: "I support the decision — but I'm going to flag this as a risk so it's on record if the timeline slips."
cards.tip: Flagging protege você e cria responsabilidade. O que não está no registro, não existe.

cards.term: Let's separate the issue from the person.
cards.ph: /lets ˈsepəreɪt ðə ˈɪʃuː frɒm ðə ˈpɜːsən/
cards.pt: Vamos separar o problema da pessoa.
cards.def: A conflict resolution principle that redirects personal disagreement toward the issue at hand.
cards.ex: "I sense some tension here — let's separate the issue from the person and focus on what the data says."
cards.tip: Princípio #1 de Harvard Negotiation. Atacar a posição, nunca a pessoa.

cards.term: I'd like to propose a structured trade-off.
cards.ph: /aɪd laɪk tə prəˈpəʊz ə ˈstrʌktʃəd treɪd ɒf/
cards.pt: Gostaria de propor uma troca estruturada, uma concessão balanceada.
cards.def: An offer to exchange concessions in a systematic way, making both sides feel the outcome is fair.
cards.ex: "I'd like to propose a structured trade-off: we move on timeline if you move on scope."
cards.tip: Troca estruturada é mais eficaz que concessão unilateral. Ambas as partes percebem valor na troca.

cards.term: This isn't about winning the argument.
cards.ph: /ðɪs ɪznt əˈbaʊt ˈwɪnɪŋ ðə ˈɑːɡjʊmənt/
cards.pt: Não se trata de ganhar o debate.
cards.def: A de-escalation statement that redirects from positional arguing to collaborative problem-solving.
cards.ex: "This isn't about winning the argument — it's about finding the option with the best risk-adjusted outcome."
cards.tip: Vencer um debate e perder a implementação é a pior vitória possível. Prefira a solução ao troféu.

cards.term: We're aligned on the goal — let's figure out the path.
cards.ph: /wiːr əˈlaɪnd ɒn ðə ɡəʊl lets ˈfɪɡər aʊt ðə pɑːθ/
cards.pt: Estamos alinhados no objetivo — vamos descobrir o caminho.
cards.def: A framing phrase that separates disagreement about method from agreement on the desired outcome.
cards.ex: "I know we disagree on the approach — but we're aligned on the goal. Let's figure out the path together."
cards.tip: Denominador comum (o objetivo) antes do debate do método reduz a resistência drasticamente.

cards.term: What does success look like for you?
cards.ph: /wɒt dʌz səkˈses lʊk laɪk fɔːr juː/
cards.pt: Como o sucesso se parece para você? Qual é seu critério de sucesso?
cards.def: An open question used to understand the stakeholder's specific definition of a successful outcome.
cards.ex: "Before we finalize the scope — what does success look like for you twelve months from now?"
cards.tip: Pergunta de ouro em discovery meetings. Alinha expectativas antes de qualquer entrega. Use sempre.

cards.term: Who else needs to be in the room for this decision?
cards.ph: /huː els niːdz tə biː ɪn ðə ruːm fɔːr ðɪs dɪˈsɪʒən/
cards.pt: Quem mais precisa estar presente para essa decisão?
cards.def: A stakeholder mapping question ensuring key decision-makers and influencers are included before committing.
cards.ex: "Before we lock in the direction — who else needs to be in the room for this decision?"
cards.tip: A decisão sem o stakeholder certo não é uma decisão — é um rascunho.

cards.term: I want to keep you in the loop on this.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə kiːp juː ɪn ðə luːp ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Quero manter você informado sobre isso.
cards.def: A proactive communication phrase signaling that someone will be regularly updated about a developing situation.
cards.ex: "This is still evolving — but I want to keep you in the loop as the situation develops."
cards.tip: Stakeholders que se sentem 'fora do loop' criam resistência. Informação proativa compra confiança.

cards.term: What's keeping you up at night?
cards.ph: /wɒts ˈkiːpɪŋ juː ʌp æt naɪt/
cards.pt: O que mais te preocupa? Qual é sua maior preocupação?
cards.def: An empathetic question that invites a stakeholder to share their deepest concerns about a situation.
cards.ex: "We can talk deliverables, but honestly — what's keeping you up at night about this integration?"
cards.tip: Acessa o emocional, não só o racional. Abre riscos que o stakeholder não mencionaria espontaneamente.

cards.term: I'd like to get your read on this.
cards.ph: /aɪd laɪk tə ɡet jɔːr riːd ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Gostaria de saber sua leitura, sua perspectiva sobre isso.
cards.def: A request for someone's interpretation, opinion, or assessment of a situation or piece of information.
cards.ex: "Before I respond to the client — I'd like to get your read on this. Does their concern seem legitimate?"
cards.tip: Menos formal que 'What is your assessment?' Convida perspectiva sem impor pressão.

cards.term: Let me manage expectations upfront.
cards.ph: /let miː ˈmænɪdʒ ekspekˈteɪʃənz ˈʌpfrʌnt/
cards.pt: Deixa eu alinhar as expectativas desde o início.
cards.def: A proactive phrase that sets realistic expectations early, preventing disappointment or misalignment later.
cards.ex: "Before we kick off — let me manage expectations upfront: the first phase will take 90 days, not 30."
cards.tip: A maioria dos conflitos com stakeholders nasce de expectativas mal alinhadas. Prevenir é mais barato que consertar.

cards.term: I'll escalate this if we can't resolve it at this level.
cards.ph: /aɪl ˈeskəleɪt ðɪs ɪf wiː kɑːnt rɪˈzɒlv ɪt æt ðɪs ˈlevəl/
cards.pt: Vou escalar isso se não conseguirmos resolver neste nível.
cards.def: A clear statement of intent to involve senior leadership if an impasse cannot be resolved at the current level.
cards.ex: "I respect your position — but I'll escalate this if we can't resolve it at this level by end of week."
cards.tip: Escalada não é fraqueza. É gestão de impasse. Dizer 'vou escalar' frequentemente resolve sem precisar escalar.

cards.term: I need your air cover on this.
cards.ph: /aɪ niːd jɔːr eər ˈkʌvər ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Preciso do seu apoio, do suporte da sua posição nisso.
cards.def: An informal request for senior-level backing that protects you or your team from interference or resistance.
cards.ex: "The restructuring will face pushback — I need your air cover to signal executive commitment."
cards.tip: Metáfora militar. 'Air cover' = patrocínio executivo ativo. Sem ele, projetos de mudança falham.

cards.term: How do you want me to communicate this to the team?
cards.ph: /haʊ duː juː wɒnt miː tə kəˈmjuːnɪkeɪt ðɪs tə ðə tiːm/
cards.pt: Como você quer que eu comunique isso para o time?
cards.def: A question that invites a stakeholder to co-own the communication strategy before it is delivered.
cards.ex: "We've agreed on the direction — how do you want me to communicate this to the team? Tone and timing matter."
cards.tip: Comunicação de mudança sem alinhamento gera ruído. Quem não alinha improvisa — e improviso cria crise.

cards.term: I want to flag a potential conflict of interest.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə flæɡ ə pəˈtenʃəl ˈkɒnflɪkt əv ˈɪntrəst/
cards.pt: Quero sinalizar um potencial conflito de interesse.
cards.def: A transparency statement that proactively discloses a situation where personal interest could influence objectivity.
cards.ex: "Before we discuss vendor selection — I want to flag a potential conflict of interest given my previous role."
cards.tip: Transparência sobre conflitos protege credibilidade. O que você não declara, alguém vai declarar por você.

cards.term: Let's agree on the communication cadence.
cards.ph: /lets əˈɡriː ɒn ðə kəˈmjuːnɪkeɪʃən ˈkeɪdəns/
cards.pt: Vamos definir a frequência de comunicação.
cards.def: An agreement on how often and through which channels updates will be shared between parties.
cards.ex: "To avoid proliferating status meetings — let's agree on the cadence: weekly email, monthly call."
cards.tip: Cadência definida reduz reuniões desnecessárias. Stakeholders ansiosos pedem mais reuniões — dê-lhes estrutura.

cards.term: I want to make sure we have executive sponsorship for this.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə meɪk ʃɔːr wiː hæv ɪɡˈzekjʊtɪv ˈspɒnsərʃɪp fɔːr ðɪs/
cards.pt: Quero garantir que temos patrocínio executivo para isso.
cards.def: A governance question ensuring that senior leadership is actively committed to and accountable for the initiative.
cards.ex: "Before we launch — I want to make sure we have executive sponsorship. Who is the named exec sponsor?"
cards.tip: 70% dos projetos de transformação falham por falta de sponsor ativo (PMI). Nomear não basta. Sponsor precisa agir.

cards.term: I'll circle back to you by end of day.
cards.ph: /aɪl ˈsɜːkəl bæk tə juː baɪ end əv deɪ/
cards.pt: Retorno para você até o final do dia.
cards.def: A commitment to follow up with someone later, typically with more information, a decision, or a completed task.
cards.ex: "I don't have the numbers — I'll circle back to you by end of day with the full breakdown."
cards.tip: Substituto elegante para 'I'll get back to you'. Implica comprometimento com prazo específico.

cards.term: As per our conversation earlier today...
cards.ph: /æz pɜːr ˈaʊər ˌkɒnvəˈseɪʃən ˈɜːliər təˈdeɪ/
cards.pt: Conforme nossa conversa de hoje mais cedo...
cards.def: An e-mail opening phrase that references a prior verbal agreement, creating a written record of what was discussed.
cards.ex: "As per our conversation earlier today, I'm sending the revised timeline and the action items agreed."
cards.tip: Cria trilha documental. Em projetos com conflito potencial, o que não está por escrito, não aconteceu.

cards.term: Please find attached the document we discussed.
cards.ph: /pliːz faɪnd əˈtætʃt ðə ˈdɒkjʊmənt wiː dɪˈskʌst/
cards.pt: Segue em anexo o documento que discutimos.
cards.def: A standard formal e-mail phrase used to introduce an attachment previously referenced in a meeting.
cards.ex: "Please find attached the document we discussed — let me know if you have questions before Thursday."
cards.tip: Clássico de e-mail executivo. Formal, claro, sem ambiguidade.

cards.term: Just looping in [name] for visibility.
cards.ph: /dʒʌst ˈluːpɪŋ ɪn neɪm fɔːr vɪˈzɪbɪlɪti/
cards.pt: Apenas incluindo [nome] para visibilidade, para manter informado.
cards.def: An e-mail phrase used when adding someone to a thread to keep them informed without requiring action from them.
cards.ex: "Just looping in Sarah for visibility — she's overseeing procurement and this touches her workstream."
cards.tip: Distingue 'para ciência' de 'para ação'. Sempre deixe claro o que é visibilidade e o que é ask.

cards.term: Could you please confirm receipt of this message?
cards.ph: /kʊd juː pliːz kənˈfɜːm rɪˈsiːt əv ðɪs ˈmesɪdʒ/
cards.pt: Pode confirmar o recebimento desta mensagem?
cards.def: A formal request for acknowledgement of receipt, used for critical decisions or sensitive communications.
cards.ex: "Given the urgency of the deadline — could you please confirm receipt of this message by noon tomorrow?"
cards.tip: Usado quando a não-resposta pode ser usada como desculpa. Cria accountability de leitura.

cards.term: I wanted to follow up on my previous e-mail.
cards.ph: /aɪ ˈwɒntɪd tə ˈfɒləʊ ʌp ɒn maɪ ˈpriːviəs ˈiːmeɪl/
cards.pt: Queria fazer um acompanhamento do meu e-mail anterior.
cards.def: A polite nudge reminding the recipient of a previous unanswered message without appearing aggressive.
cards.ex: "I wanted to follow up on my e-mail from Tuesday — could you share your thoughts by end of week?"
cards.tip: Nunca diga 'I haven't heard back' (soa agressivo). 'Follow up' é a forma profissional de cobrar resposta.

cards.term: Please revert with your comments by [date].
cards.ph: /pliːz rɪˈvɜːt wɪð jɔːr ˈkɒments baɪ deɪt/
cards.pt: Por favor, retorne com seus comentários até [data].
cards.def: A formal request for feedback or review by a specific deadline — common in consulting and formal correspondence.
cards.ex: "The draft is attached — please revert with your comments by Friday so we can finalize before submission."
cards.tip: 'Revert' nesse sentido é inglês formal (UK). No contexto americano: 'please respond with your comments'.

cards.term: I'm writing to request your input on...
cards.ph: /aɪm ˈraɪtɪŋ tə rɪˈkwest jɔːr ˈɪnpʊt ɒn/
cards.pt: Escrevo para solicitar sua contribuição sobre...
cards.def: A formal e-mail opening that states the purpose clearly and signals that a specific type of response is expected.
cards.ex: "I'm writing to request your input on the proposed governance model before we present it to the board."
cards.tip: E-mails eficazes têm propósito claro na primeira linha. Quem esconde o ask no final não recebe resposta.

cards.term: For reference, I'm including the previous thread below.
cards.ph: /fɔːr ˈrefrəns aɪm ɪnˈkluːdɪŋ ðə ˈpriːviəs θred bɪˈləʊ/
cards.pt: Para referência, incluo o fio de conversa anterior abaixo.
cards.def: A phrase indicating that context from a previous email exchange is included for the recipient's reference.
cards.ex: "For reference, I'm including the previous thread — the decision on Phase 2 was made on March 12th."
cards.tip: Cria trilha de auditoria. Em conflitos sobre 'quem disse o quê', o thread é sua proteção.

cards.term: Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have questions.
cards.ph: /pliːz dəʊnt ˈhezɪteɪt tə riːtʃ aʊt ɪf juː hæv ˈkwestʃənz/
cards.pt: Não hesite em entrar em contato se tiver dúvidas.
cards.def: A standard e-mail closing that signals openness and invites the recipient to ask follow-up questions.
cards.ex: "The report is attached — please don't hesitate to reach out if you have questions before the presentation."
cards.tip: Encerramento padrão em e-mails profissionais formais. Cria abertura sem criar obrigação de resposta imediata.

cards.term: We need to move the needle on this.
cards.ph: /wiː niːd tə muːv ðə ˈniːdəl ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Precisamos gerar progresso real nisso.
cards.def: To make meaningful, measurable progress on a problem or initiative, rather than just discussing it.
cards.ex: "We've been talking about NPS for six months. We need to actually move the needle on this before Q3."
cards.tip: Implica urgência e orientação a resultado. Se tudo é 'move the needle', nada é prioritário.

cards.term: Let's not boil the ocean.
cards.ph: /lets nɒt bɔɪl ðə ˈəʊʃən/
cards.pt: Não vamos tentar resolver tudo de uma vez.
cards.def: A warning against trying to solve every problem simultaneously or making a project unnecessarily complex.
cards.ex: "Let's not boil the ocean — what are the three levers with the most impact? Focus there."
cards.tip: Maturidade estratégica: saber o que NÃO fazer é tão importante quanto o que fazer.

cards.term: What is the highest-leverage action we can take right now?
cards.ph: /wɒt ɪz ðə ˈhaɪɪst ˈliːvərɪdʒ ˈækʃən wiː kæn teɪk raɪt naʊ/
cards.pt: Qual é a ação de maior alavancagem que podemos tomar agora?
cards.def: A prioritization question seeking the single action with the greatest impact relative to the effort required.
cards.ex: "We have limited bandwidth — what is the highest-leverage action we can take right now to stabilize this?"
cards.tip: Leverage = impacto ÷ esforço. A pergunta força a escolha do 20% que gera 80% do resultado.

cards.term: This is a burning platform.
cards.ph: /ðɪs ɪz ə ˈbɜːnɪŋ ˈplætfɔːm/
cards.pt: Essa situação exige ação imediata — não há tempo para hesitar.
cards.def: A metaphor from crisis management indicating the cost of inaction exceeds the risk of acting.
cards.ex: "I know change is hard — but this is a burning platform. If we don't act this quarter, the window closes."
cards.tip: Urgência existencial, não operacional. Força a saída da zona de conforto da inação.

cards.term: We're running out of runway.
cards.ph: /wiːr ˈrʌnɪŋ aʊt əv ˈrʌnweɪ/
cards.pt: Estamos ficando sem tempo, sem margem para agir.
cards.def: A phrase indicating that available time, budget, or resources are nearing exhaustion.
cards.ex: "The Board expects results in 60 days — we're running out of runway and need to prioritize ruthlessly."
cards.tip: Runway = caixa ou tempo restante. Do universo de startups, agora comum em qualquer gestão de crise.

cards.term: What's the critical path here?
cards.ph: /wɒts ðə ˈkrɪtɪkəl pɑːθ hɪər/
cards.pt: Qual é o caminho crítico aqui?
cards.def: A project management question seeking the sequence of dependent tasks that determines minimum completion time.
cards.ex: "Before we assign resources — what's the critical path? Which tasks cannot slip without affecting launch?"
cards.tip: Caminho crítico tem zero float. Qualquer atraso nele = atraso total. Priorize o crítico, não o urgente.

cards.term: We need to triage this.
cards.ph: /wiː niːd tə ˈtriːɑːʒ ðɪs/
cards.pt: Precisamos priorizar e classificar isso.
cards.def: The process of sorting issues by urgency and impact to focus limited resources optimally.
cards.ex: "We have twelve open issues and a team of three — we need to triage this and focus on what will kill the project."
cards.tip: Metáfora médica. Em projetos: urgência × impacto = prioridade. Não tudo que grita é crítico.

cards.term: Let's focus on what we can control.
cards.ph: /lets ˈfəʊkəs ɒn wɒt wiː kæn kənˈtrəʊl/
cards.pt: Vamos focar no que está sob nosso controle.
cards.def: A pragmatic redirect that separates controllable factors from external constraints and channels energy productively.
cards.ex: "There's a lot of uncertainty — let's focus on what we can control and execute with discipline."
cards.tip: Stoic leadership principle. Em crise, equipes que focam no controlável performam melhor.

cards.term: We're burning daylight on this.
cards.ph: /wiːr bɜːnɪŋ ˈdeɪlaɪt ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Estamos perdendo tempo precioso nisso.
cards.def: An informal expression indicating time is being wasted on a topic that isn't progressing.
cards.ex: "We've debated the naming for thirty minutes. We're burning daylight — let's vote and move on."
cards.tip: Mais direto que 'we're wasting time'. Transmite urgência sem soar agressivo.

cards.term: What's the minimum viable version of this?
cards.ph: /wɒts ðə ˈmɪnɪməm ˈvaɪəbəl ˈvɜːʃən əv ðɪs/
cards.pt: Qual é a versão mínima viável disso?
cards.def: A question seeking the simplest version that still delivers core value — borrowed from startup methodology.
cards.ex: "We don't have time for the full solution — what's the minimum viable version that still moves the needle?"
cards.tip: MVP não é só para software. Em gestão, é a arte de entregas parciais com valor total. Feito bate perfeito.

cards.term: What's the cost of doing nothing?
cards.ph: /wɒts ðə kɒst əv ˈduːɪŋ ˈnʌθɪŋ/
cards.pt: Qual é o custo de não fazer nada?
cards.def: A decision-forcing question that quantifies the risk of inaction, countering the bias toward the status quo.
cards.ex: "Before you defer this — what's the cost of doing nothing for another six months?"
cards.tip: Status quo bias é real. Quantificar o custo da inação é a forma mais eficaz de criar urgência de decisão.

cards.term: We're looking at a two-speed approach.
cards.ph: /wiːr ˈlʊkɪŋ æt ə tuː spiːd əˈprəʊtʃ/
cards.pt: Estamos pensando em uma abordagem em dois ritmos, paralela.
cards.def: A strategy that runs two workstreams at different paces — quick wins and longer-term transformation.
cards.ex: "Two-speed approach: stabilize cash flow in 90 days while redesigning the operating model."
cards.tip: Equilibra urgência de curto prazo com mudança estrutural. Comum em projetos de transformação.

cards.term: I want to challenge you on that assumption.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə ˈtʃælɪndʒ juː ɒn ðæt əˈsʌmpʃən/
cards.pt: Quero te desafiar nessa premissa.
cards.def: A coaching phrase that invites a person to re-examine a belief or assumption they are taking for granted.
cards.ex: "I want to challenge you on that assumption — what evidence do you have that the timeline is achievable?"
cards.tip: Coaches não dão respostas. Dão perguntas que fazem a pessoa encontrar a resposta. Desenvolvimento real.

cards.term: What's stopping you from making that call?
cards.ph: /wɒts ˈstɒpɪŋ juː frɒm ˈmeɪkɪŋ ðæt kɔːl/
cards.pt: O que está te impedindo de tomar essa decisão?
cards.def: A direct coaching question that surfaces the real barrier — fear, uncertainty, or lack of authority — behind hesitation.
cards.ex: "You know what the right answer is — what's stopping you from making that call?"
cards.tip: Identifica o bloqueio real (medo, falta de dados, política) que a hesitação disfarça. Direto ao ponto.

cards.term: Here is my honest assessment.
cards.ph: /hɪər ɪz maɪ ˈɒnɪst əˈsesmənt/
cards.pt: Aqui está minha avaliação honesta.
cards.def: A preface signaling that what follows is candid, unfiltered feedback rather than diplomatic softening.
cards.ex: "You asked for my perspective — here is my honest assessment: the plan is solid but the team isn't ready."
cards.tip: Prefaciar com 'honest assessment' cria permissão para candor. Feedback sem aviso pode soar como ataque.

cards.term: What would you do if you had full authority?
cards.ph: /wɒt wʊd juː duː ɪf juː hæd fʊl ɔːˈθɒrɪti/
cards.pt: O que você faria se tivesse total autoridade?
cards.def: A powerful coaching question that removes political constraints and reveals a person's genuine preferred course of action.
cards.ex: "What would you do if you had full authority and no pushback? Then — what stops you from proposing that?"
cards.tip: Remove o filtro político e revela a resposta real. Depois: 'então o que impede você de propor isso?'

cards.term: I want to give you some candid feedback.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə ɡɪv juː sʌm ˈkændɪd ˈfiːdbæk/
cards.pt: Quero te dar um feedback sincero.
cards.def: A transparent opening that signals the feedback will be direct and honest, preparing the recipient to listen openly.
cards.ex: "I want to give you some candid feedback — your analysis is strong but the communication of it needs work."
cards.tip: 'Candid' implica coragem de dizer o que precisa ser dito, não o que é confortável.

cards.term: You are closer to this than I am — what's your instinct?
cards.ph: /juː ɑːr ˈkləʊsər tə ðɪs ðæn aɪ æm wɒts jɔːr ˈɪnstɪŋkt/
cards.pt: Você está mais perto disso do que eu — qual é o seu instinto?
cards.def: A humble leadership phrase that defers to the person with more direct context, validating their expertise.
cards.ex: "You've been working this account for two years — you are closer to this than I am. What's your instinct?"
cards.tip: Liderança situacional: quem tem mais contexto deve liderar a análise. Humildade intelectual como ferramenta.

cards.term: How can I help remove the barriers for you?
cards.ph: /haʊ kæn aɪ help rɪˈmuːv ðə ˈbæriərz fɔːr juː/
cards.pt: Como posso ajudar a remover os obstáculos para você?
cards.def: A servant leadership phrase that offers active support for a team member facing obstacles to their work.
cards.ex: "You've flagged two blockers this week — how can I help remove the barriers for you?"
cards.tip: O trabalho do líder é remover obstáculos, não adicionar tarefas. Isso muda o tipo de liderança que você exerce.

cards.term: What's the lesson learned here?
cards.ph: /wɒts ðə ˈlesən lɜːnd hɪər/
cards.pt: Qual é a lição aprendida aqui?
cards.def: A retrospective question that transforms a mistake, setback, or success into institutional knowledge.
cards.ex: "We missed the deadline — I'm not here to blame. What's the lesson learned that we apply next time?"
cards.tip: Lições aprendidas sem documentação desaparecem. Documentadas, viram ativos organizacionais.

cards.term: I want to recognize the work you did on this.
cards.ph: /aɪ wɒnt tə ˈrekəɡnaɪz ðə wɜːk juː dɪd ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Quero reconhecer o trabalho que você fez nisso.
cards.def: A deliberate phrase of specific recognition that names the work done, rather than a generic compliment.
cards.ex: "Before we move on — I want to recognize the work you did on this. The stakeholder mapping was exceptional."
cards.tip: Reconhecimento específico vale 10x mais que genérico. 'Great job' é vago. 'Your analysis was decisive' é concreto.

cards.term: Let's do a pre-mortem on this.
cards.ph: /lets duː ə priː ˈmɔːtəm ɒn ðɪs/
cards.pt: Vamos fazer um pré-mortem nisso.
cards.def: A prospective technique where a team imagines the project has failed and works backward to identify what went wrong.
cards.ex: "Before we launch — let's do a pre-mortem. Imagine it's 6 months from now and the project failed. Why?"
cards.tip: Técnica de Gary Klein. Contraria pensamento de grupo. Perspectiva do fracasso revela riscos que otimismo esconde.

cards.term: What's your biggest concern about this approach?
cards.ph: /wɒts jɔːr ˈbɪɡɪst kənˈsɜːn əˈbaʊt ðɪs əˈprəʊtʃ/
cards.pt: Qual é sua maior preocupação com essa abordagem?
cards.def: An open question designed to surface hidden objections or risks that a person may not volunteer spontaneously.
cards.ex: "Everyone seems supportive — what's your biggest concern about this approach? I want the honest read."
cards.tip: Convida dissidência explicitamente. Grupos que não nomeiam preocupações constroem consenso frágil.

cards.term: How would you frame this to senior leadership?
cards.ph: /haʊ wʊd juː freɪm ðɪs tə ˈsiːniər ˈliːdəʃɪp/
cards.pt: Como você enquadraria isso para a alta liderança?
cards.def: A coaching question that develops executive communication skills by asking someone to think from the top.
cards.ex: "The analysis is ready — how would you frame this to senior leadership? What's the headline and the ask?"
cards.tip: Desenvolve comunicação executiva no time. Quem consegue fazer isso vira consultor, não analista.

cards.term: I'm going to be straight with you.
cards.ph: /aɪm ˈɡəʊɪŋ tə biː streɪt wɪð juː/
cards.pt: Vou ser direto com você.
cards.def: A candor signal that prepares the listener for frank, unfiltered information, establishing psychological safety.
cards.ex: "I'm going to be straight with you — this proposal won't land well with the board in its current form."
cards.tip: Aviso de candor cria receptividade. Dizer 'vou ser direto' antes de ser direto reduz o choque do conteúdo.

cards.term: What's your theory of change here?
cards.ph: /wɒts jɔːr ˈθɪəri əv tʃeɪndʒ hɪər/
cards.pt: Qual é a sua teoria de mudança aqui?
cards.def: A strategic question asking someone to articulate the causal logic between proposed actions and the desired outcome.
cards.ex: "You're proposing a training program — what's your theory of change? How does training translate to behavior change?"
cards.tip: Separa ação de impacto. Muitas intervenções existem sem teoria de mudança clara. Exigir uma é rigor.

cards.term: Let's make sure the incentives are aligned.
cards.ph: /lets meɪk ʃɔːr ðə ɪnˈsentɪvz ɑːr əˈlaɪnd/
cards.pt: Vamos garantir que os incentivos estão alinhados.
cards.def: A systems-thinking phrase ensuring that reward structures motivate the desired behaviors across all parties.
cards.ex: "The plan looks good on paper — let's make sure the incentives are aligned so people actually behave as expected."
cards.tip: Comportamento segue incentivo. O plano que ignora incentivos reais falha na implementação, sempre.

cards.term: We need to build a coalition around this.
cards.ph: /wiː niːd tə bɪld ə kəʊˈɪlɪʃən əˈraʊnd ðɪs/
cards.pt: Precisamos construir uma coalizão em torno disso.
cards.def: A political strategy of building a critical mass of supporters before presenting or implementing a major initiative.
cards.ex: "The idea is strong — but we need to build a coalition around this before taking it to the executive committee."
cards.tip: Mudança organizacional não é decidida em reuniões — é preparada nos corredores. Coalizão antes de arena.
